name: AutoScraper
tagline: Learn web scraping rules from example data, no selectors required
seoTitle: AutoScraper - Smart Python Web Scraper by Example
metaDescription: AutoScraper is a lightweight Python library that learns scraping rules from sample data you point at, then reuses them on new pages. No CSS/XPath selectors.
overview: AutoScraper is a Python library that builds web scrapers from examples instead of hand-written selectors. You give it a URL (or raw HTML) plus a short list of sample values you want from the page, and it figures out the rules that match those values. It then returns the similar elements on that page.

Once a scraper is built, you can reuse the same learned object on new URLs that share the same layout, such as other product, profile, or listing pages on the same site. The wanted data can be text, a URL, or any HTML tag value, and you can save the model to a file and load it again later.

It fits the web scraping and crawling category as a fast, dependency-light option for developers who want results quickly without writing and maintaining CSS or XPath selectors. It is compatible with Python 3 and passes parameters through to the requests module, so proxies and custom headers work out of the box.
features: Learns scraping rules automatically from a list of example values you want from a page
Works from a live URL or from raw HTML content you already have
Reuses a built scraper on new pages with similar structure via get_result_similar and get_result_exact
Matches text, URLs, or any HTML tag value as the wanted data
Passes through custom requests parameters such as proxies and headers via request_args
Saves and loads built models to and from a file for later reuse

gettingStarted.intro: Install AutoScraper from PyPI, then build a scraper by pointing it at a URL and a few sample values you want.

steps.heading: Install from PyPI
steps.body: AutoScraper is compatible with Python 3. Install the latest release with pip.

steps.heading: Build a scraper from an example
steps.body: Pass a URL and a wanted_list of sample values. AutoScraper learns the rules and returns the similar elements on the page.
steps.code:
from autoscraper import AutoScraper

url = 'https://stackoverflow.com/questions/2081586/web-scraping-with-python'

# Add one or more candidate values you want from the page
wanted_list = ["What are metaclasses in Python?"]

scraper = AutoScraper()
result = scraper.build(url, wanted_list)
print(result)

steps.heading: Reuse the scraper on new pages
steps.body: Apply the same learned scraper to other pages with a similar layout to pull matching content.

steps.heading: Save and load the model
steps.body: Persist a built scraper to a file and load it back later instead of rebuilding it.
steps.code:
# Give it a file path
scraper.save('yahoo-finance')

# Later
scraper.load('yahoo-finance')

useCases: Quickly extract repeated items like post titles, prices, or links from a page without writing CSS or XPath selectors
Build a reusable scraper for one site layout, then run it across many similar pages such as product or profile pages
Pull a fixed set of fields (for example about text, star count, and an issues link) in a known order with get_result_exact
Wrap a learned scraper in a small API or script to turn a website into structured data
